sourceNote: Audited code: the Shipment workflow of github.com/temporalio/reference-app-orders-go (app/shipment/workflows.go, vendored under source/). Observable state is the workflow's own StatusQuery projection: the shipment status string. NOTE the audit's central question: the carrier-update loop applies the signal's status VERBATIM — the contract's dataDomain therefore deliberately includes a value outside the documented enum, so the checker can explore what the code actually does with it. AUDIT NOTE: the first contract draft projected only {status}; the model check exposed that 'pending' is then ambiguous (never-booked vs carrier-regressed-to-pending have different behavior), making the machine not a function of the projection — Polygraph's contract-error class. The projection was widened with the `booked` flag (the BookShipment activity's completion, an observable event).

stateKeys.name: status
stateKeys.type: string — documented values: 'pending' | 'booked' | 'dispatched' | 'delivered'; the code does not enforce this set (that is finding S1)

stateKeys.name: booked
stateKeys.type: boolean — whether the BookShipment activity has completed (the carrier-update loop only drains after booking)

dataFields.status: string — the carrier's reported status, applied to the shipment VERBATIM. The workflow's receive loop exits (workflow completes) when the status becomes 'delivered'.

specialRules.name: booking-first
specialRules.note: BOOKED (the BookShipment activity completing) is only applicable while status == 'pending'; it moves the shipment to 'booked' and notifies the requestor.

specialRules.name: carrier-update-verbatim
specialRules.note: CARRIER_UPDATE (applicable when booked == true and status != 'delivered') sets status to the signal's value VERBATIM — including values outside the documented enum, duplicates, and regressions (e.g. 'dispatched' -> 'booked', or even back to 'pending'). Each update notifies the requestor. When the value is 'delivered' the receive loop exits and the workflow completes (terminal).

specialRules.name: no-updates-before-booking-or-after-delivery
specialRules.note: A CARRIER_UPDATE before booking (booked == false: the loop is not yet draining — the signal buffers, observably nothing changes at the sample instant) or after 'delivered' (workflow completed) yields post == pre in the observable projection.

noOpRule: An action that does not apply in the current state yields post == pre.
